title: Chapter Twenty-Two: The Middle Dantian
content:
## JEONG YEON-SHIN

Yu Hyeon charged, teeth gritted.

A strange energy swirled around Yeon-shin's left hand. The eyes of Cheon Ju and the Mount Hua masters widened.

Invisible true energy waves burst forth -- blooming like a series of flowers blossoming in a row. Force striking force, creating another wave of energy. A higher-level martial art that did not exist until this moment, inspired by the Falling Petal Palm and made into something else entirely.

The third form of the Eternal Blossom Fist.

Blossoming Fist.

Impacts as powerful as Advancing Thunder rained down in droves. As if brushing off the dust that had settled on Yu Hyeon's martial robes.

He collapsed without even drawing his sword. A thin layer of dust rose like fog.

Lowering his hand, Yeon-shin collected his thoughts.

*The martial arts of the Nine Sects are highly refined. Worthy of being imbued with the history of the orthodox sects.*

Three forms. Yu Hyeon -- the youngest disciple of the Mount Hua Sect Leader, said to be the most promising -- had fallen in three.

Yeon-shin thought it was only natural. The level was different from the start. To face Lightning Flash, who was considered one of the top White-rank warriors, at least a master on the verge of becoming a Plum Blossom Swordsman had to step up.

"Bring him," Cheon Ju said, grave as a funeral.

A Mount Hua warrior picked up Yu Hyeon. The gazes sweeping over Yeon-shin were filled with shock.

Murmurs erupted from the spectators.

"Three stances! Who *is* that young man?"

"Desolate Sect was raising a dragon," Cheon Ju said, turning back to Ma Jin. "How much more will they shake the martial world in the future?"

"That's an excessive exaggeration. If they don't touch the common people, Desolate Sect won't move."

"That plausible justification never changes." Cheon Ju shook his head, then looked at Yeon-shin. "You said your name is Jeong Yeon-shin?"

"Aye."

"I witnessed good martial arts. The fate of Mount Hua's martial arts couldn't have possibly reached you. I didn't know such martial arts existed in the world." His voice carried respect now, and something harder to name. "If a situation arises where you inevitably have to leave Desolate Sect, know that Mount Hua will treat you as an honoured guest."

"Sect Leader!" Ma Jin pulled Yeon-shin back and stepped forward. "Don't be ridiculous. You've crossed the line."

"I admit that greed took precedence. It was unseemly." Cheon Ju dipped his head. "Because such shining talent is right before our eyes, unparalleled in the world."

Yeon-shin simply stood still.

*It seems difficult for you to endure,* came Baek Mi-ryeo's telepathic message.

He slightly nodded. He had exhausted almost all of his true energy. The burden on his dantian was immense. In the Eternal Blossom Fist, the proportion of true energy used to create strong force was high. Exerting force with every posture meant exactly that.

*In real combat, I'll have to return to the sword.*

He got a room at the inn. Those who followed sent changed gazes -- eyes that seemed to be looking at an amazing creature. He had no particular feelings.

Because the time they lived in was different.

As soon as he entered, Baek Mi-ryeo barged through the door, dragging Cheong Myeong by the scruff of his neck.

"You in particular need to open your Middle Dantian quickly," she said, sitting cross-legged. Cheong Myeong sprawled and half-lay down behind her. "If you lack internal energy, you'll have to at least supplement your stamina."

"Middle Dantian?"

"You must not know about its ability. The moment you open it, your internal energy will become more solid. Shaolin's Vajra Immovable Divine Art is a representative martial art that cultivates the Middle Dantian."

"Internal energy becomes solid?"

Cheong Myeong chimed in. "It means the efficiency improves. You've heard stories of masters who fought for seven days and nights without getting tired? They cultivated the Middle Dantian. The flow of true energy becomes firm -- you can unleash even large stances several times. Even if you take a powerful blow, the flow isn't easily cut off."

"That's probably the reason Cheong Myeong crossed swords with Namgung's First Sword," Baek Mi-ryeo added, pointing. "Otherwise it doesn't make sense, no matter how powerful this man's martial arts are."

Cheong Myeong smiled brightly. Tilted his head at an angle.

"How do I open it?" Yeon-shin asked.

"Cultivate the Lower Dantian for a long time. Circulate your energy focusing on your upper body. It will naturally open."

"Is there no faster way? Sister."

The word came out without thought. He had to quickly acquire all means to become stronger. Impatient in martial arts and achievements, the form of address came as it may.

Even saying it himself, he flinched.

"Sister...?"

Baek Mi-ryeo's cold gaze softened. Her cheeks turned slightly red. For a moment, her lips seemed to quiver.

*That Bewitching Demon Sword?*

Like a true master, she quickly took a deep breath and regained composure. Yeon-shin could not help but wonder if she had a deceased younger sibling. At the same time, he realised he did not know anything about the lives of any of the Radiant Demon Wing warriors.

*I still don't know anyone.*

Baek Mi-ryeo cleared her throat. "First, you need to hold powerful energy within your body..."

The explanation continued. The problem ultimately came down to true energy. Just as the lower dantian naturally opened through long cultivation, the middle dantian was no different. She said that clumsy techniques would only be poison.

*Just as I opened my upper dantian from birth. Damn constitution.*

He swallowed a sigh and went downstairs. He decided to think about the middle dantian later.

*Focus on accumulating power. I can get stronger without it.*

---

The inn was crowded, the smell of food overwhelming. Ma Jin and Heon Won-chang sat at conspicuously empty seats -- the chilling scar and strong aura prevented anyone from approaching.

Yeon-shin walked through the crowd and sat.

"Is this alright?" he asked. "Discussing the mission in the open?"

Ma Jin smiled. "It's no secret that Mount Hua is searching for the culprit with eyes wide open. The whereabouts of martial artists from famous sects are bound to be known in detail. Rather, doing it openly can reduce unexpected casualties among commoners."

"Make noise in the east and attack in the west," Ma Jin continued. "That's what we're going to do. Mount Hua's martial arts are too conspicuous in Shaanxi. So we will smash the signboards of the evil sects connected with Thieving Ghost, one by one. We draw attention. Mount Hua's Drifting Dark Fragrance footwork lets them secretly infiltrate and inspect the sects."

"Did you say Thieving Ghost?"

Baek Mi-ryeo stood abruptly. An energy like tempering a sword rose in spirals from her body. Her sharpened gaze flashed killing intent.

"The one who stole your secret manual," Ma Jin said. "Isn't it a good thing?"

"You had your martial arts manual stolen?" Heon Won-chang's eyes widened.

"There are circumstances," Baek Mi-ryeo said, and closed her mouth. But her aura was different now -- it came to the skin like a burning wildfire.

They finished the meal in silence and went up to Ma Jin's room. He made eye contact with each of them.

"Five sects are the targets. I'll take two."

"I'll do one alone," Yeon-shin said.

No one showed surprise. Ma Jin asked:

"Do you want solo credit? The risk is high."

"I'll handle it."

"Dealing with a group alone is another matter. Especially a sect with martial arts. Think of corpses piling up with every sword strike. The scent of blood can take away even a master's reason. Group battles have the most variables."

"That's also something I need to experience."

Ma Jin studied him. Then nodded.

"Your target is the Praying Mantis Sect in Lianhu County. I've heard they're very vicious. They don't hesitate to kidnap women." He met Yeon-shin's eyes. "Solo annihilation of the entire sect. Wipe them out and come back."

---
